title: Launchpad Environment
description: Configuration file for a Launchpad environment. Defines databases, credentials, APIs, MCP servers, scripts, hooks, and system prompts for Claude Code sessions.

name.description: Display name for this environment (e.g. "Local Dev", "Staging").

description.description: Short description shown in the sidebar and quick-pick menu.

icon.description: VS Code codicon name for the sidebar icon (e.g. "tools", "beaker", "warning", "cloud"). See https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/icons-in-labels for the full list.

tabName.description: Custom terminal tab name. Defaults to "Claude: <name>". Git context (branch, PR, worktree) is appended automatically.

claude.description: Claude Code CLI settings for this environment.

dangerouslySkipPermissions.description: Skip all permission prompts (--dangerously-skip-permissions). Use only in trusted local environments.

model.description: Override the Claude model for this environment (e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-opus-4-6").

allowedTools.description: Restrict which tools Claude can use. Each entry is a tool name or pattern (e.g. "Bash(git:*)").

environmentVariables.description: Environment variables passed to the Claude CLI process.

systemPrompt.description: Free-form markdown injected into Claude's system prompt via --append-system-prompt. Use this for environment-specific instructions, warnings, or context.

variables.description: Environment variables set in the terminal and included in Claude's system prompt.

databases.description: Database connections available in this environment. Shown in the sidebar with copy actions for connection strings and credentials.

accounts.description: Test or service accounts. Passwords support secret references and are resolved at launch time.

apis.description: API endpoints relevant to this environment. Used for health checks (HTTP HEAD) and included in Claude's system prompt.

mcpServers.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to start alongside Claude. Written to a temp config and passed via --mcp-config.

scripts.description: Runnable scripts shown in the sidebar with play/stop controls. Can open in split terminals.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks that run at specific points during the launch flow.

preLaunch.description: Commands to run before launching Claude. Executed sequentially with a progress notification. The launch aborts on failure unless continueOnError is set.

sections.description: Freeform markdown sections included in Claude's system prompt. Keys become headings, values become the body. Use for deployment notes, known issues, team conventions, etc.

examples.Known Issues:
- Redis cache may be stale after deploy
- OAuth tokens expire every 24h

secretReference.description:
A value or secret reference. Supported formats:
- Plain text: "myvalue"
- 1Password CLI: "op://vault/item/field"
- .env file: "env://.env.local/VAR_NAME"
- OS environment variable: "$VAR_NAME"
- macOS Keychain / Linux secret-tool: "keychain://service/account"

Secrets are resolved at launch time only and never written to disk.

variable.description: An environment variable set in the terminal session.

value.description: Variable value. Supports secret references (op://, env://, $VAR, keychain://).

secret.description: If true, the value is masked in the UI and treated as sensitive. Set this when the value is a secret reference.

database.description: A database connection. Supports health checks (TCP connect, Redis PING) and credential copy actions.

type.description: Database engine type. Used for icon selection, connection string format, and health check method.

port.description: Port number. Common defaults: postgres=5432, mysql=3306, redis=6379, mongo=27017.

database.description: Database name (or Redis DB number as a string, e.g. "0").

password.description: Password. Supports secret references (op://, env://, $VAR, keychain://). Resolved at launch time.

connectionString.description: Full connection string (e.g. "postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db"). If provided, used by the Copy Connection String action. Otherwise, the extension builds one from host/port/database/username/password.

notes.description: Additional notes shown in Claude's system prompt (e.g. "Read replica at db-ro.example.com").

account.description: A test or service account with credentials.

password.description: Password. Supports secret references (op://, env://, $VAR, keychain://). Resolved at launch time.

notes.description: Additional notes (e.g. "Has active subscription, test card 4242...").

api.description: An API endpoint. Used for health checks (HTTP HEAD) and included in Claude's system prompt.

auth.description: How to authenticate (e.g. "Bearer token from /auth/login", "API key in X-Api-Key header").

mcpServer.description: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. Launchpad writes these to a temp config file and passes it to Claude via --mcp-config.

name.description: Server name used as the key in the MCP config (e.g. "postgres", "sentry", "filesystem").

env.description: Environment variables for the MCP server process. Values support secret references.

docker.description: Docker Compose configuration. Manages container lifecycle when launching and closing environments.

composeFile.description: Path to docker-compose file(s). Defaults to "docker-compose.yml".

services.description: Specific services to manage. If omitted, all services in the compose file are used.

upOnLaunch.description: Run `docker compose up -d` before launching Claude.

downOnExit.description: Run `docker compose down` when the terminal closes.

waitHealthy.description: Wait for services to report healthy before launching Claude.

waitTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds when waiting for healthy services.

script.description: A runnable script shown in the sidebar with play/stop controls.

command.description: Shell command to execute (e.g. "npm run dev", "pytest").

cwd.description: Working directory relative to workspace root. Defaults to workspace root.

split.description: If true, opens in a split terminal alongside the active terminal instead of a new tab.

hook.description: A shell command that runs as part of the launch lifecycle.

cwd.description: Working directory. Defaults to workspace root.

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds. The hook is killed if it exceeds this.

continueOnError.description: If true, the launch continues even if this hook fails. Otherwise, failure aborts the launch.
